questions.question:

(GATE CSE 1993)

questions.question:
Consider the undirected unweighted graph G. Let a breadth-first traversal of G be done starting from a node r . Let d(r,u) and d(r,v) be the lengths of the shortest paths form r to u and v respectively in G. If u is visited before v during the breadth-first traversal, which of the following statements is correct ?

(GATE CSE 2001)

questions.question:
Choose the correct alternatives (More than one may be correct).
A graph is planar if and only if,

(GATE CSE 1990)
options:
A.

It does not contain subgraphs homeomorphic to \(k_{5}\) and \(k_{3, 3}\).

B.

It does not contain subgraphs isomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\).

C.

It does not contain a subgraph isomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\)

D.

It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\).
questions.correct_answer:
D.

It does not contain a subgraph homeomorphic to \(k_{5}\) or \(k_{3, 3}\).
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
